commit.message:
Fix confetti animation bug: replace addTimer with addEvent

The showCelebration() function was using this.time.addTimer() which
does not exist in Phaser 3. This caused confetti particles to remain
static instead of animating. Fixed by using the correct API
this.time.addEvent() which is used consistently throughout the file.

Fixes #981

commit.message:
Implement wall collision detection with Phaser arcade physics (#940)

- Enable arcade physics in Phaser game config (office-init.js)
- Add physics bodies for walls (top, bottom, left, right perimeter)
- Add physics bodies for meeting room walls with door gaps
- Add physics bodies for desks and furniture
- Add physics bodies to agent and sub-agent sprites
- Set up colliders between agents and walls
- Integrate with existing collision-avoidance.js system

The implementation creates invisible static physics bodies that match
the visual walls and obstacles. Agents now have circular physics bodies
that collide with walls, preventing them from walking through solid
objects while maintaining compatibility with the existing pathfinding
and collision avoidance system.

commit.message:
clang-21 on macos fixes (#126235)

Addresses various build failures when compiling with the latest Xcode.

```
% clang --version
Apple clang version 21.0.0 (clang-2100.0.123.102)
Target: arm64-apple-darwin25.4.0
Thread model: posix
InstalledDir: /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/bin
```

See
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/119706#issuecomment-4145857457

Clang 21 changes for `-Wc++-keyword` -
https://releases.llvm.org/21.1.0/tools/clang/docs/ReleaseNotes.html#id276
Partially related https://github.com/unicode-org/icu/pull/2913

commit.message:
fix: Remove duplicate pattern listener registration causing interaction visual flickering (issue #939)

Root cause: setupPatternListeners() was registering the same event listeners twice:
1. Immediately if officeBackend was ready
2. Again with a 2-second delayed retry

This caused duplicate event handling for every interaction pattern, leading to
flickering, flashing, and unstable visual feedback. The fix:
- Add _patternListenersSetup flag to prevent duplicate method calls
- Extract listener wiring into a reusable function
- Ensure listeners are registered exactly once per scene instance
- Keep the retry logic for web mode but only call wireListeners once

This ensures a single source of truth from backend → IPC → renderer.

* docs: add cross-cutting error diagnostic observability requirements

Add a new requirement to each component spec mandating that errors at
user-facing or operator-visible boundaries include: the failed operation,
triggering input/parameters, underlying subsystem error, and actionable
guidance where possible.

New requirements:
- GW-1307  Error diagnostic observability (gateway)
- ND-1014  Error diagnostic observability (node)
- MD-0506  Error diagnostic observability (modem)
- PT-1215  Error diagnostic observability (pairing tool)

Appendix indexes updated in all files that have them.

commit.message:
gateway: sustained health poll failures trigger modem reconnect (#564)

* gateway: sustained health poll failures trigger modem reconnect

After N consecutive poll_status() failures (default 3), the health
monitor now logs at ERROR and returns \	rue\ to signal the caller that
a modem reconnect is needed. The failure counter resets on any
successful poll.

Changes:
- Add \DEFAULT_MAX_HEALTH_POLL_FAILURES\ constant (3)
- Add \max_consecutive_failures\ parameter to \spawn_health_monitor\
- Change return type from \JoinHandle<()>\ to \JoinHandle<bool>\
- Update gateway binary call site to pass the new parameter and
  handle the reconnect signal
- Add T-1104c integration test
- Update GW-1103 requirements, design, and validation docs

Fixes #557

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* address review: validate max_consecutive_failures=0 and use saturating_add

- Return false (disable monitor) when max_consecutive_failures is 0,
  mirroring the existing interval-is-zero guard
- Use saturating_add(1) to prevent u32 overflow on the failure counter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(gateway): health monitor returns false when disabled (max_failures=0)

When `max_consecutive_failures` is 0, the health monitor now returns
`false` (no reconnect needed) instead of completing immediately and
inadvertently triggering the reconnect path.

commit.message:
feat(dpar): KV ストアから Redis Pub/Sub イベントバスに再設計、MySQL 8.4 に更新

## MySQL
- docker-compose.yml: mysql:8.0 → mysql:8.4

## dpar 設計変更（KV ストア → Pub/Sub イベントバス）
### Domain
- entities/dpar.py: Dpar(key/value/ttl) → Event(channel/payload/event_id/timestamp)
- repositories/dpar.py: IDparRepository(get/set/delete/keys) → IEventBus(publish/subscribe)

### Application
- use_cases/dpar.py: DparUseCase → EventUseCase(publish/subscribe)

### Infrastructure
- redis.py: get_redis()（同期/health用）に加えて get_async_redis()（非同期/Pub/Sub用）を追加
- repositories/dpar.py: RedisDparRepository → RedisEventBus（redis.asyncio 使用）

### Presentation
- schemas/dpar.py: EventPublish(payload) / EventResponse(channel/payload/event_id/timestamp)
- routers/v3/dpar.py:
  - POST /{channel}/publish: Redis PUBLISH
  - GET /{channel}/subscribe: Redis SUBSCRIBE + Server-Sent Events ストリーミング

## テスト
- test_root_endpoint.py: get_async_redis DI オーバーライドを追加（fakeredis.aioredis）
- test_v3_dpar.py: 新規追加（publish エンドポイント 3 ケース）
- 28 tests passed

## Docs
- docs/architecture.md: MySQL 8.4・dpar Pub/Sub 設計・リクエストフロー図を更新

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
